sections.title: Listening
sections.description: 20 questions • Approximately 20 minutes • You will hear each recording only once

parts.title: Part 1: Buying a New Phone
parts.instructions: Listen to the conversation. Answer questions 1-5.

script.narrator:
You will hear a man buying a phone. First, look at questions 1 to 5.

[Pause]

Now listen and answer questions 1 to 5.

conversation.speaker: male
conversation.text: Yes, I want a new phone.

conversation.speaker: female
conversation.text: Good. What colour do you like?

conversation.speaker: female
conversation.text: Okay. This phone is blue. It is two hundred pounds.

conversation.speaker: male
conversation.text: Two hundred pounds. Okay. Does it have a good camera?

conversation.speaker: female
conversation.text: Yes, the camera is very good.

conversation.speaker: male
conversation.text: Good. I want to take photos. I will buy it.

conversation.speaker: female
conversation.text: Thank you, Ben. Do you want a phone case?

questions.questionText: What does the man want to buy?
What colour phone does he like?
What colour case does he want?

parts.title: Part 2: Using a Computer
parts.instructions: Listen to the teacher. Answer questions 6-10.

script.narrator:
Now listen to a teacher. Look at questions 6 to 10.

[Pause]

Now listen and answer questions 6 to 10.

conversation.speaker: female
conversation.text: Good morning, class. Today we use the computer. The computer is on the table. First, press the blue button. The computer is on. Now, look at the screen. Use the mouse. Click on the internet picture. It is blue. Now you can search. You can watch videos. You can read news. Do not press the red button. The red button closes the computer. Thank you.

questions.questionText: What do you use to click?
What can you do on the internet?
What does the red button do?

passages.title: Passage 1: My New Mobile Phone
passages.content: I have a new mobile phone. It is black. It is small. I can take photos with my phone. I can call my friends. I can send messages. I can play music. I like my phone. It is very good.

questions.questionText: What can you do with the phone?
The writer thinks the phone is...

passages.title: Passage 2: Computers at School
passages.content: Students use computers at school. They learn with computers. They type words. They read on the screen. They find information on the internet. The teacher helps the students. Computers are useful for learning.

tasks.title: Writing Task 1
tasks.instructions: You should spend about 20 minutes on this task. Write about your mobile phone. Answer these questions: What colour is it? What do you use it for? Why do you like it? Write at least 50 words.
tasks.description: Write a short, simple paragraph about your phone.

tasks.title: Writing Task 2
tasks.instructions: You should spend about 20 minutes on this task. Do you like using computers? Why or why not? Write at least 50 words.
tasks.description: Write a short, simple opinion about computers.

parts.title: Part 1: Introduction and Interview
parts.instructions: The examiner will ask you questions about yourself.
sampleQuestions: Do you have a mobile phone?

parts.title: Part 2: Individual Long Turn
parts.instructions: Talk about a technology you use. You have one minute to prepare.

taskCard.topic: Describe a technology you use every day.

parts.title: Part 3: Simple Questions
parts.instructions: The examiner will ask you more questions about technology.

metadata.author: IELTS Beginner Practice
metadata.notes: This is a simplified IELTS-style test for absolute beginners (approx. CEFR A1 level), using the theme of technology. The language, topics, and tasks are designed to be very accessible.
